title: Egypt: Signing of Grant Agreement by EU for Green Ammonia Project at Ain Sokhna
content: Minister of Planning, Economic Development and International Cooperation Rania El-Mashat participated on Tuesday, February 10, 2026, in the conference organised by the European Union on "The Future of Sustainable Energy in Egypt 2040: Cooperation for Shared Prosperity", attended by Dr Mahmoud Esmat, Minister of Electricity and Renewable Energy; Ms Angelina Eichhorst, Ambassador of the European Union to Cairo; Eng Walid Gamal El-Din, Chairman of the Suez Canal Economic Zone Authority; the ambassadors of Spain and France; and representatives of European banks. During the conference a grant agreement was signed by the European Union, managed by the European Investment Bank, worth â‚¬90 million to boost investments in Egyptâ€™s power grid and build renewable energy capacity, and a â‚¬35 million grant to the Norwegian company Scatec for the green ammonia project at Ain Sokhna. In her remarks, Dr El-Mashat stressed that the transition to sustainable, secure and efficient energy systems is one of the main drivers of inclusive development in Egypt. It forms part of the national narrative for comprehensive development aimed at achieving sustainable economic growth, enhancing the competitiveness of the economy, and meeting climate action objectives.
author: Analysts Account
